messages.content: Build me a task management dashboard with a data table backend. I want to be able to add tasks with a title, description, and deadline. When I add a task, an AI agent should automatically assign a priority (urgent/high/medium/low) and a category based on the description. Also pull my Google Calendar events for the week and show them alongside tasks so I can see my workload. Include filters for priority and category.
Creating the `tasks` data table now. I'll kick off the workflows in parallel as soon as it's ready.

args.task:
Build a workflow named "Task Manager – Add Task" that does the following:

1. **Webhook trigger** — POST at path `add-task`. Accepts JSON body: { title, description, deadline }
2. **Claude AI node** — Use the Anthropic credential "Anthropic account" (id: 609q7ZROTvgTvuzV). Send a prompt asking Claude to analyze the task title and description and return ONLY a valid JSON object with two fields:
   - "priority": one of "urgent", "high", "medium", or "low"
   - "category": a short free-form category label (e.g. "Work", "Health", "Finance", "Learning", "Personal", etc.)
   The prompt should be clear that it must return ONLY raw JSON, no markdown, no explanation.
3. **Code node** — Parse Claude's text response to extract the JSON. Build the full task record: { title, description, deadline, priority, category, created_at: current ISO datetime }
4. **n8n data table insert** — Insert the record into the data table with ID `mxxIO5f6AzVSIuYr` (table name: "tasks")
5. **Respond to Webhook** — Return 200 with the saved task record as JSON (include all fields)

Make the workflow active after saving.

result.result: Workflow build started (task: build-LV5_u_-d). Acknowledge briefly and move on.

args.task:
Build a workflow named "Task Manager – List Tasks" that does the following:

1. **Webhook trigger** — GET at path `list-tasks`. May receive optional query parameters: `priority` and `category`
2. **n8n data table read** — Read all rows from the data table with ID `mxxIO5f6AzVSIuYr` (table name: "tasks"). Get all columns: title, description, deadline, priority, category, created_at
3. **Code node** — Filter the rows based on query params from the webhook:
   - If `priority` query param is present and non-empty, keep only rows where priority matches (case-insensitive)
   - If `category` query param is present and non-empty, keep only rows where category matches (case-insensitive)
   - If neither param is present, return all rows
   - Sort results by created_at descending
4. **Respond to Webhook** — Return 200 with the filtered array as JSON

Make the workflow active after saving.

args.task:
Build a workflow named "Task Manager – Calendar Events" that does the following:

1. **Webhook trigger** — GET at path `calendar-events`
2. **Code node** — Calculate the start and end of the current week (Monday 00:00:00 to Sunday 23:59:59) in ISO 8601 format with timezone offset
3. **Google Calendar node** — Use credential "Google Calendar account" (id: 5MjqGu3t0YdRw9uf, type: googleCalendarOAuth2Api). Fetch events from the primary calendar for the date range calculated above. Get fields: id, summary, start, end
4. **Code node** — Map the events into a clean array of objects: { id, summary, start, end } where start and end are ISO datetime strings. Handle both all-day events (date only) and timed events (dateTime).
5. **Respond to Webhook** — Return 200 with the events array as JSON

Make the workflow active after saving.

results.description: Serving a single-page web application from an n8n webhook — dashboard, admin panel, interactive tool, form with rich UI
results.patternHint: Write HTML to chunks/page.html, then in src/workflow.ts use readFileSync + JSON.stringify to embed it in a Code node. Use multiple webhooks for page + API routes. Respond with text/html for pages, allEntries for JSON APIs.

suggestedNodes.name: n8n-nodes-base.webhook
suggestedNodes.note: Set responseMode to "responseNode". Use one webhook per route (e.g. GET /app for page, GET /app/items for API, POST /app/items/add for mutations). Access POST body via $json.body

suggestedNodes.name: n8n-nodes-base.respondToWebhook
suggestedNodes.note: For HTML pages: respondWith "text", responseBody via expression, Content-Type: text/html header. For JSON APIs: respondWith "allEntries"

suggestedNodes.name: n8n-nodes-base.aggregate
suggestedNodes.note: Use aggregateAllItemData to collect multiple DataTable rows into a single array before the Code node that builds the HTML page

suggestedNodes.name: n8n-nodes-base.code
suggestedNodes.note: Builds the final HTML by replacing a __DATA_PLACEHOLDER__ token with base64-encoded data. The HTML template is loaded via readFileSync + JSON.stringify in the SDK code — NEVER embed large HTML directly in jsCode

suggestedNodes.name: n8n-nodes-base.dataTable
suggestedNodes.note: PREFERRED for SPA data storage — no external config needed. Use resource: "row" with get/insert/update operations for CRUD API routes

results.builderHintMessage: For workflow data storage, DataTable with upsert avoids duplicates. Use Google Sheets when spreadsheet collaboration is specifically needed.

plan.goal: Build a task management dashboard with a data table backend, AI-powered priority/category classification via Claude, Google Calendar integration, and a filterable web UI.
plan.summary: Three workflows + one data table power the dashboard: (1) a task submission webhook that calls Claude to classify priority & category then saves to the data table; (2) a read/filter API that returns tasks with optional priority/category filters; (3) a Google Calendar fetch API that returns this week's events. A single-page HTML dashboard (served by a fourth webhook) ties everything together with an add-task form, task list with filters, and a calendar sidebar.
assumptions: AI provider: Anthropic Claude (credential: 'Anthropic account')
Google Calendar credential: 'Google Calendar account' (googleCalendarOAuth2Api)
Categories are assigned freely by Claude based on task description
Priority values: urgent / high / medium / low
Data table stores: id, title, description, deadline, priority, category, created_at
Dashboard is a self-contained HTML page served by an n8n webhook
Tasks are never deleted from the UI (read + create only for now)
dataContracts: Task record: { id, title, description, deadline, priority (urgent|high|medium|low), category (string), created_at }
Add-task request: POST { title, description, deadline }
List-tasks response: [{ id, title, description, deadline, priority, category, created_at }]
Calendar response: [{ id, summary, start, end }]
acceptanceCriteria: Submitting a task via the dashboard form saves it with AI-assigned priority and category
Tasks list is filterable by priority and category without page reload
Google Calendar events for the current week appear in the sidebar
Dashboard is accessible at a single webhook URL

phases.title: Create Tasks Data Table
phases.description: Create the n8n data table that stores all task records with the required schema.
phases.objective: Provision the tasks data table with columns: title, description, deadline, priority, category, created_at.
phases.deliverable: A data table named 'tasks' with the correct schema ready to accept records.

verification.successCriteria: Data table 'tasks' exists with all required columns
verification.expectedOutcome: Table visible in list-data-tables with correct column definitions

phases.title: Build Task Submission Workflow (AI Classification)
phases.description: Webhook that accepts POST { title, description, deadline }, calls Claude to assign priority and category, then inserts the record into the tasks data table. Returns the saved task as JSON.
phases.objective: POST /webhook/add-task → Claude classifies → saved to data table → 200 JSON response.
phases.deliverable: Active workflow 'Task Manager – Add Task' with webhook at /add-task

verification.successCriteria: Workflow runs successfully and inserts a row with AI-assigned priority and category
verification.expectedOutcome: Execution succeeds; data table contains the new task row

phases.title: Build Task List API Workflow
phases.description: Webhook that accepts GET with optional query params ?priority=&category= and returns filtered tasks from the data table as JSON.
phases.objective: GET /webhook/list-tasks?priority=high&category=Work → returns matching task array.
phases.deliverable: Active workflow 'Task Manager – List Tasks' with webhook at /list-tasks

verification.successCriteria: Workflow returns a JSON array of tasks (empty array is acceptable if table is empty)
verification.expectedOutcome: Execution succeeds with 200 and JSON body

phases.title: Build Google Calendar Fetch Workflow
phases.description: Webhook that accepts GET and returns this week's Google Calendar events (Monday–Sunday) as a JSON array.
phases.objective: GET /webhook/calendar-events → returns array of { id, summary, start, end } for current week.
phases.deliverable: Active workflow 'Task Manager – Calendar Events' with webhook at /calendar-events

verification.successCriteria: Workflow returns a JSON array (may be empty if no events this week)
verification.expectedOutcome: Execution succeeds with 200 and JSON body

phases.title: Build Dashboard UI Workflow
phases.description: Webhook that serves a self-contained HTML page. The page has: an Add Task form (title, description, deadline), a task list with priority/category filter dropdowns, and a weekly calendar sidebar. All data is fetched client-side from the three API webhooks built in phases 2–4.
phases.objective: GET /webhook/dashboard → returns full HTML dashboard page.
phases.deliverable: Active workflow 'Task Manager – Dashboard' with webhook at /dashboard

verification.successCriteria: Workflow is active and the dashboard URL returns a valid HTML page
verification.expectedOutcome: Browser loads the dashboard at http://localhost:5678/webhook/dashboard

Dashboard is a self-contained HTML page served by an n8n webhook
